forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 9f80
+project: local-review
+title: 'Incremental review working: skip files unchanged since last review via .validators tracking'
+---
+## What
+
+`review working` baselines against `HEAD`, but in a `/finish` fix-loop `HEAD` never moves (changes aren't committed between passes), so every re-review re-scopes the **whole** uncommitted change set × every validator — a fresh ~10-min full-tree pass that re-finds nits on files the latest fix never touched. This is the dominant review-churn cost observed in the calcutron qwen eval.
+
+Fix: make `review working` **incremental**, enforced inside the review tool/engine (NOT in the `/finish` skill) so every caller — the loop, the CLI, parallel subagents — benefits and shares state. Track, per reviewed file, a content+rules **context hash** on disk under a `.validators/` directory; when resolving working changes, subtract files whose context hash is unchanged since they were last reviewed.
+
+### Tracking layout (`.validators/`)
+- One **YAML** entry per reviewed source file (NOT JSON, NOT a shared manifest), path-mirrored for debuggability: a file `src/error.rs` → `.validators/src/error.rs.yaml`.
+- Entry contents (plaintext + component hashes, so "why did this re-review?" is eyeball-able):
+  ```yaml
+  path: src/error.rs
+  context_hash: blake3:<combined>
+  content_hash: blake3:<file content>
+  rules_hash: blake3:<rules>
+  reviewed_at: 2026-06-14T18:40:00Z
+  ```
+- `context_hash = H(relative_path ‖ file_content ‖ rules_hash)`. Changing the path, the content, OR the rules changes the context hash ⇒ the file is re-reviewed.
+- **`rules_hash` is global for v1** — a hash of all loaded validator rule bodies. (Per-file applicable-rules hashing is a later refinement; a rule edit forcing a full re-sweep is acceptable.)
+- **Atomic writes** (write temp + rename) so concurrent `sah` processes (subagents) never tear each other's state. Concurrent writers compute the same hash and write the same value — idempotent; races only ever cause a redundant re-review, never incorrectness.
+- `.validators/` is transient working-tree bookkeeping — add it to `.gitignore`; never commit it.
+
+### Flow changes
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — after `resolve_working` builds the candidate set (all changed + untracked vs HEAD, unchanged), apply a new tracking filter: for each candidate, compute its current `context_hash` and **subtract** it if `.validators/<path>.yaml` exists with an equal `context_hash`. Per-file `before` stays HEAD (so the semantic diff still shows the real change, not just the last fix) — the tracking only decides *inclusion*.
+- After a review **completes successfully**, upsert the tracking entry for every reviewed file — **regardless of findings**. Open findings persist in the kanban task checklist; an unchanged file with an open finding is correctly NOT re-scanned (it can't have been fixed, and re-scanning only re-derives the same finding). When the implementer edits it, content changes ⇒ hash changes ⇒ re-review.
+- **Zero survivors ⇒ short-circuit to a clean/empty report** without spinning up the fleet (no prime/fork cost for a no-op pass).
+- **Force-full escape hatch**: `review working` honors a `force`/`all` signal (and/or an absent/cleared `.validators/`) to ignore tracking and review the whole set. Document that `review working` is now default-narrow once a baseline exists.
+
+### Cross-file duplication safety
+Subtracting unchanged files from the review target set stays safe: the duplicates probe runs over the whole-repo corpus regardless of scope, and a change can only *introduce* a dup in the file it changed — which survives the subtraction. A dup purely between two unchanged files was already caught when they were last changed.
+
+Files to modify/create:
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/` — new tracking module (e.g. `tracking.rs`): `context_hash`, read/subtract, atomic upsert, path-mirrored YAML under `.validators/`.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — wire the subtract filter into `resolve_working`; thread a `use_tracking`/`force` flag.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/review_op.rs` — record reviewed files after a successful review; pass the force flag through.
+- `.gitignore` — add `.validators/`.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] After a `review working` pass, `.validators/<path>.yaml` entries exist for every reviewed file with plaintext path + `context_hash`/`content_hash`/`rules_hash`/`reviewed_at`.
+- [ ] A second `review working` with **no file changes** subtracts everything and short-circuits to a clean/empty report without running the fleet.
+- [ ] Editing one file changes only that file's `context_hash`; the next `review working` reviews **only** that file (others subtracted).
+- [ ] Changing the rules (global `rules_hash`) invalidates all entries ⇒ next `review working` reviews the full set.
+- [ ] A file with an open finding that is left unchanged is NOT re-reviewed on the next pass (its entry was written despite the finding).
+- [ ] The force/all hatch reviews the full set ignoring tracking.
+- [ ] Entries are written atomically (temp + rename); `.validators/` is gitignored; format is YAML.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/tracking.rs` unit tests: `context_hash` changes on path/content/rules change and is stable otherwise; YAML round-trips; atomic upsert overwrites in place.
+- [ ] `scope.rs` test: candidate set minus tracking entries — unchanged file subtracted, changed file kept, new file kept, rules-changed ⇒ none subtracted.
+- [ ] Integration test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators` (or review_op): seed a repo, run review (records entries), run again unchanged ⇒ zero-survivor clean short-circuit; touch one file ⇒ only that file reviewed.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::` and review_op tests → green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing tracking/scope/integration tests first, then implement.
\ No newline at end of file

 `review working` baselines against `HEAD`, but in a `/finish` fix-loop `HEAD` never moves (changes aren't committed between passes), so every re-review re-scopes the **whole** uncommitted change set × every validator — a fresh ~10-min full-tree pass that re-finds nits on files the latest fix never touched. +Fix: make `review working` **incremental**, enforced inside the review tool/engine (NOT in the `/finish` skill) so every caller — the loop, the CLI, parallel subagents — benefits and shares state. Track, per reviewed file, a content+rules **context hash** on disk under the `.validators/` directory; when resolving working changes, subtract files whose context hash is unchanged since they were last reviewed.
+
+### Directory layout (`.validators/`)
+`.validators/` is the project validators directory and MAY hold **committed** project rule config — so it must NOT be blanket-ignored at the repo root. The ephemeral hash tracking lives in a **subdirectory**, and a `.gitignore` *inside* `.validators/` ignores just that subdirectory:

-### Tracking layout (`.validators/`)
-- One **YAML** entry per reviewed source file (NOT JSON, NOT a shared manifest), path-mirrored for debuggability: a file `src/error.rs` → `.validators/src/error.rs.yaml`.
-- Entry contents (plaintext + component hashes, so "why did this re-review?" is eyeball-able):
+```
+.validators/
+  .gitignore            # generated by review init: ignores the tracking subdir, e.g. `reviewed/`
+  reviewed/             # ephemeral per-file context-hash tracking (gitignored)
+    src/error.rs.yaml
+    src/parser.rs.yaml
+  <committed project rule files stay tracked>
+```
+
+- **`review`'s init generates `.validators/.gitignore`** (content ignores the tracking subdir, e.g. a line `reviewed/`). Committed project validator config under `.validators/` stays tracked. Do NOT add `.validators/` to the repo-root `.gitignore`.
+- If init hasn't run, the tracking writer **lazily ensures `.validators/.gitignore` exists** (create-if-missing) before writing entries, so the hash dir is never accidentally committed.
+
+### Tracking entries (`.validators/reviewed/`)
+- One **YAML** entry per reviewed source file (NOT JSON, NOT a shared manifest), path-mirrored for debuggability: `src/error.rs` → `.validators/reviewed/src/error.rs.yaml`.
+- Entry contents — plaintext + component hashes, so "why did this re-review?" is eyeball-able (confirmed desired):
   ```yaml
   path: src/error.rs
   context_hash: blake3:<combined>
@@ -25,36 +40,37 @@

 - **`rules_hash` is global for v1** — a hash of all loaded validator rule bodies. (Per-file applicable-rules hashing is a later refinement; a rule edit forcing a full re-sweep is acceptable.)
 - **Atomic writes** (write temp + rename) so concurrent `sah` processes (subagents) never tear each other's state. Concurrent writers compute the same hash and write the same value — idempotent; races only ever cause a redundant re-review, never incorrectness.
-- `.validators/` is transient working-tree bookkeeping — add it to `.gitignore`; never commit it.

 ### Flow changes
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — after `resolve_working` builds the candidate set (all changed + untracked vs HEAD, unchanged), apply a new tracking filter: for each candidate, compute its current `context_hash` and **subtract** it if `.validators/<path>.yaml` exists with an equal `context_hash`. Per-file `before` stays HEAD (so the semantic diff still shows the real change, not just the last fix) — the tracking only decides *inclusion*.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — after `resolve_working` builds the candidate set (all changed + untracked vs HEAD, unchanged), apply a new tracking filter: for each candidate, compute its current `context_hash` and **subtract** it if `.validators/reviewed/<path>.yaml` exists with an equal `context_hash`. Per-file `before` stays HEAD (so the semantic diff still shows the real change, not just the last fix) — the tracking only decides *inclusion*.
 - After a review **completes successfully**, upsert the tracking entry for every reviewed file — **regardless of findings**. Open findings persist in the kanban task checklist; an unchanged file with an open finding is correctly NOT re-scanned (it can't have been fixed, and re-scanning only re-derives the same finding). When the implementer edits it, content changes ⇒ hash changes ⇒ re-review.

-- **Force-full escape hatch**: `review working` honors a `force`/`all` signal (and/or an absent/cleared `.validators/`) to ignore tracking and review the whole set. Document that `review working` is now default-narrow once a baseline exists.
+- **Force-full escape hatch**: `review working` honors a `force`/`all` signal (and/or a cleared `.validators/reviewed/`) to ignore tracking and review the whole set. Document that `review working` is now default-narrow once a baseline exists.

 ### Cross-file duplication safety
 Subtracting unchanged files from the review target set stays safe: the duplicates probe runs over the whole-repo corpus regardless of scope, and a change can only *introduce* a dup in the file it changed — which survives the subtraction. A dup purely between two unchanged files was already caught when they were last changed.

 Files to modify/create:
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/` — new tracking module (e.g. `tracking.rs`): `context_hash`, read/subtract, atomic upsert, path-mirrored YAML under `.validators/`.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/` — new tracking module (e.g. `tracking.rs`): `context_hash`, read/subtract, atomic upsert, path-mirrored YAML under `.validators/reviewed/`, and ensure-`.validators/.gitignore`.
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — wire the subtract filter into `resolve_working`; thread a `use_tracking`/`force` flag.
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/review_op.rs` — record reviewed files after a successful review; pass the force flag through.
-- `.gitignore` — add `.validators/`.
+- review init path (where the project `.validators/` is set up) — generate `.validators/.gitignore` ignoring the tracking subdir.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] After a `review working` pass, `.validators/<path>.yaml` entries exist for every reviewed file with plaintext path + `context_hash`/`content_hash`/`rules_hash`/`reviewed_at`.
+- [ ] `review` init generates `.validators/.gitignore` that ignores the tracking subdir (e.g. `reviewed/`); committed config under `.validators/` stays tracked; `.validators/` is NOT added to the repo-root `.gitignore`.
+- [ ] If init hasn't run, writing tracking lazily creates `.validators/.gitignore` before the first entry.
+- [ ] After a `review working` pass, `.validators/reviewed/<path>.yaml` entries exist for every reviewed file with plaintext path + `context_hash`/`content_hash`/`rules_hash`/`reviewed_at`.
 - [ ] A second `review working` with **no file changes** subtracts everything and short-circuits to a clean/empty report without running the fleet.
 - [ ] Editing one file changes only that file's `context_hash`; the next `review working` reviews **only** that file (others subtracted).
 - [ ] Changing the rules (global `rules_hash`) invalidates all entries ⇒ next `review working` reviews the full set.
 - [ ] A file with an open finding that is left unchanged is NOT re-reviewed on the next pass (its entry was written despite the finding).

-- [ ] Entries are written atomically (temp + rename); `.validators/` is gitignored; format is YAML.
+- [ ] Entries are written atomically (temp + rename); format is YAML.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/tracking.rs` unit tests: `context_hash` changes on path/content/rules change and is stable otherwise; YAML round-trips; atomic upsert overwrites in place.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/tracking.rs` unit tests: `context_hash` changes on path/content/rules change and is stable otherwise; YAML round-trips; atomic upsert overwrites in place; ensure-`.validators/.gitignore` is created with the tracking-subdir ignore line and is idempotent.
 - [ ] `scope.rs` test: candidate set minus tracking entries — unchanged file subtracted, changed file kept, new file kept, rules-changed ⇒ none subtracted.
-- [ ] Integration test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators` (or review_op): seed a repo, run review (records entries), run again unchanged ⇒ zero-survivor clean short-circuit; touch one file ⇒ only that file reviewed.
+- [ ] Integration test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators` (or review_op): seed a repo, run review (records entries + writes `.validators/.gitignore`), run again unchanged ⇒ zero-survivor clean short-circuit; touch one file ⇒ only that file reviewed.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::` and review_op tests → green.

 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — wire the subtract filter into `resolve_working`; thread a `use_tracking`/`force` flag.
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/review_op.rs` — record reviewed files after a successful review; pass the force flag through.
-- review init path (where the project `.validators/` is set up) — generate `.validators/.gitignore` ignoring the tracking subdir.
+- `.gitignore` — add `.validators/`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `review` init generates `.validators/.gitignore` that ignores the tracking subdir (e.g. `reviewed/`); committed config under `.validators/` stays tracked; `.validators/` is NOT added to the repo-root `.gitignore`.
-- [ ] If init hasn't run, writing tracking lazily creates `.validators/.gitignore` before the first entry.
-- [ ] After a `review working` pass, `.validators/reviewed/<path>.yaml` entries exist for every reviewed file with plaintext path + `context_hash`/`content_hash`/`rules_hash`/`reviewed_at`.
+- [ ] After a `review working` pass, `.validators/<path>.yaml` entries exist for every reviewed file with plaintext path + `context_hash`/`content_hash`/`rules_hash`/`reviewed_at`.
 - [ ] A second `review working` with **no file changes** subtracts everything and short-circuits to a clean/empty report without running the fleet.
 - [ ] Editing one file changes only that file's `context_hash`; the next `review working` reviews **only** that file (others subtracted).
 - [ ] Changing the rules (global `rules_hash`) invalidates all entries ⇒ next `review working` reviews the full set.
 - [ ] A file with an open finding that is left unchanged is NOT re-reviewed on the next pass (its entry was written despite the finding).
 - [ ] The force/all hatch reviews the full set ignoring tracking.
-- [ ] Entries are written atomically (temp + rename); format is YAML.
+- [ ] Entries are written atomically (temp + rename); `.validators/` is gitignored; format is YAML.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/tracking.rs` unit tests: `context_hash` changes on path/content/rules change and is stable otherwise; YAML round-trips; atomic upsert overwrites in place; ensure-`.validators/.gitignore` is created with the tracking-subdir ignore line and is idempotent.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/tracking.rs` unit tests: `context_hash` changes on path/content/rules change and is stable otherwise; YAML round-trips; atomic upsert overwrites in place.
 - [ ] `scope.rs` test: candidate set minus tracking entries — unchanged file subtracted, changed file kept, new file kept, rules-changed ⇒ none subtracted.
-- [ ] Integration test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators` (or review_op): seed a repo, run review (records entries + writes `.validators/.gitignore`), run again unchanged ⇒ zero-survivor clean short-circuit; touch one file ⇒ only that file reviewed.
+- [ ] Integration test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators` (or review_op): seed a repo, run review (records entries), run again unchanged ⇒ zero-survivor clean short-circuit; touch one file ⇒ only that file reviewed.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::` and review_op tests → green.

 ## Workflow

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -13,22 +13,22 @@
 Fix: make `review working` **incremental**, enforced inside the review tool/engine (NOT in the `/finish` skill) so every caller — the loop, the CLI, parallel subagents — benefits and shares state. Track, per reviewed file, a content+rules **context hash** on disk under the `.validators/` directory; when resolving working changes, subtract files whose context hash is unchanged since they were last reviewed.

 ### Directory layout (`.validators/`)
-`.validators/` is the project validators directory and MAY hold **committed** project rule config — so it must NOT be blanket-ignored at the repo root. The ephemeral hash tracking lives in a **subdirectory**, and a `.gitignore` *inside* `.validators/` ignores just that subdirectory:
+`.validators/` is the project validators directory and MAY hold **committed** project rule config — so it must NOT be blanket-ignored at the repo root. The ephemeral hash tracking lives in the **`.hashes/`** subdirectory (source tree mirrored under it), and a `.gitignore` *inside* `.validators/` ignores just that subdirectory:

 ```
 .validators/
-  .gitignore            # generated by review init: ignores the tracking subdir, e.g. `reviewed/`
-  reviewed/             # ephemeral per-file context-hash tracking (gitignored)
+  .gitignore            # generated by review init: ignores `.hashes/`
+  .hashes/              # ephemeral per-file context-hash tracking (gitignored), source tree mirrored
     src/error.rs.yaml
     src/parser.rs.yaml
   <committed project rule files stay tracked>
 ```

-- **`review`'s init generates `.validators/.gitignore`** (content ignores the tracking subdir, e.g. a line `reviewed/`). Committed project validator config under `.validators/` stays tracked. Do NOT add `.validators/` to the repo-root `.gitignore`.
+- **`review`'s init generates `.validators/.gitignore`** with a line ignoring `.hashes/`. Committed project validator config under `.validators/` stays tracked. Do NOT add `.validators/` to the repo-root `.gitignore`.
 - If init hasn't run, the tracking writer **lazily ensures `.validators/.gitignore` exists** (create-if-missing) before writing entries, so the hash dir is never accidentally committed.

-### Tracking entries (`.validators/reviewed/`)
-- One **YAML** entry per reviewed source file (NOT JSON, NOT a shared manifest), path-mirrored for debuggability: `src/error.rs` → `.validators/reviewed/src/error.rs.yaml`.
+### Tracking entries (`.validators/.hashes/`)
+- One **YAML** entry per reviewed source file (NOT JSON, NOT a shared manifest), path-mirrored for debuggability: `src/error.rs` → `.validators/.hashes/src/error.rs.yaml`.
 - Entry contents — plaintext + component hashes, so "why did this re-review?" is eyeball-able (confirmed desired):
   ```yaml
   path: src/error.rs
@@ -42,24 +42,24 @@
 - **Atomic writes** (write temp + rename) so concurrent `sah` processes (subagents) never tear each other's state. Concurrent writers compute the same hash and write the same value — idempotent; races only ever cause a redundant re-review, never incorrectness.

 ### Flow changes
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — after `resolve_working` builds the candidate set (all changed + untracked vs HEAD, unchanged), apply a new tracking filter: for each candidate, compute its current `context_hash` and **subtract** it if `.validators/reviewed/<path>.yaml` exists with an equal `context_hash`. Per-file `before` stays HEAD (so the semantic diff still shows the real change, not just the last fix) — the tracking only decides *inclusion*.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — after `resolve_working` builds the candidate set (all changed + untracked vs HEAD, unchanged), apply a new tracking filter: for each candidate, compute its current `context_hash` and **subtract** it if `.validators/.hashes/<path>.yaml` exists with an equal `context_hash`. Per-file `before` stays HEAD (so the semantic diff still shows the real change, not just the last fix) — the tracking only decides *inclusion*.
 - After a review **completes successfully**, upsert the tracking entry for every reviewed file — **regardless of findings**. Open findings persist in the kanban task checklist; an unchanged file with an open finding is correctly NOT re-scanned (it can't have been fixed, and re-scanning only re-derives the same finding). When the implementer edits it, content changes ⇒ hash changes ⇒ re-review.
 - **Zero survivors ⇒ short-circuit to a clean/empty report** without spinning up the fleet (no prime/fork cost for a no-op pass).
-- **Force-full escape hatch**: `review working` honors a `force`/`all` signal (and/or a cleared `.validators/reviewed/`) to ignore tracking and review the whole set. Document that `review working` is now default-narrow once a baseline exists.
+- **Force-full escape hatch**: `review working` honors a `force`/`all` signal (and/or a cleared `.validators/.hashes/`) to ignore tracking and review the whole set. Document that `review working` is now default-narrow once a baseline exists.

 ### Cross-file duplication safety
 Subtracting unchanged files from the review target set stays safe: the duplicates probe runs over the whole-repo corpus regardless of scope, and a change can only *introduce* a dup in the file it changed — which survives the subtraction. A dup purely between two unchanged files was already caught when they were last changed.

 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — wire the subtract filter into `resolve_working`; thread a `use_tracking`/`force` flag.
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/review_op.rs` — record reviewed files after a successful review; pass the force flag through.
-- review init path (where the project `.validators/` is set up) — generate `.validators/.gitignore` ignoring the tracking subdir.
